perturbation.original_text: MusclePharm hereby engages Endorser and Endorser promises and agrees to hold himself available to use, evaluate, advertise and promote certain MusclePharm Products, as may be reasonably requested by MusclePharm in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein on a world-wide basis. Endorser also agrees to the use on a world-wide basis (as specified pursuant to Section 6 below and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement), during the Term, of his Name and Appearance Rights to advertise and promote the business of MusclePharm, its Products, and the Licensed Products.
perturbation.changed_text: MusclePharm hereby enlists Endorser and Endorser promises and agrees to hold himself available to use, evaluate, advertise and promote certain MusclePharm commodities, as may be reasonably requested by MusclePharm in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein on a world-wide basis. Endorser also agrees to the use on a world-wide basis (as specified pursuant to Section 6 below and subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement), during the Term, of his Name and Appearance Rights to advertise and promote the business of MusclePharm, its commodities, and the Licensed Products.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Products" to "commodities" creates confusion, as "Products" is defined later in section 2(b) while "commodities" is not. This creates ambiguity regarding what exactly Endorser is obligated to promote. This is a key term that is being changed in the first paragraph of section 2, that creates confusion on what product the endorser, is been obligated to endorse. It is contradictory, due to the product definition later on in section 2(b).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Products. As used in this Agreement, "Products" shall mean dietary supplements manufactured within the fifty states of the United States of America; provided, however, MusclePharm shall not produce during the Term (and any renewal Term, if any) any diet pills and/or sexual enhancement products; provided, further, that fat burning products, Shred Matrix and Live Shredded products and products that increase testosterone levels currently produced by MusclePharm as of the date hereof shall be part of the definition of Products for the purpose of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the term "Products," which is explicitly defined in section 2(b) of the agreement. The changed text replaces "Products" with "commodities," which is undefined in the agreement. Because "Products" is already defined, using "commodities" introduces ambiguity and contradicts the intended meaning as established by the definition in 2(b). This is an example of misaligned terminology that creates a direct conflict, making the terms of the agreement unclear and therefore unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term, MusclePharm also shall have the right to use, worldwide, Endorser's oral or written endorsements of Products and Licensed Products, or paraphrases thereof, to promote MusclePharm, its business, Products, and Licensed Products. Endorser shall have the right to approve such oral or written endorsements and the use thereof, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term, MusclePharm also shall have the right to use, worldwide, Endorser's oral or written endorsements of Products and Licensed Products, or paraphrases thereof, to promote MusclePharm, its business, dietary supplements, and Licensed Products. Endorser shall have the right to approve such oral or written endorsements and the use thereof, such approval not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Products" to "dietary supplements" in Section 4(c), we create a contradiction. The original contract allows MusclePharm to use Endorser's endorsements for 'Products,' broadly defined. However, by specifying 'dietary supplements' in this section, it suggests that endorsements might be limited to only dietary supplements, despite the broader definition of 'Products' established in Section 2(b). This is because the modified sentence now reads as a specific example that limits the scope of 'Products'. This is not to be confused as the original defintion of products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Products. As used in this Agreement, "Products" shall mean dietary supplements manufactured within the fifty states of the United States of America; provided, however, MusclePharm shall not produce during the Term (and any renewal Term, if any) any diet pills and/or sexual enhancement products; provided, further, that fat burning products, Shred Matrix and Live Shredded products and products that increase testosterone levels currently produced by MusclePharm as of the date hereof shall be part of the definition of Products for the purpose of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Products" to "dietary supplements" in Section 4(c), when read in conjunction with the original definition of "Products" in Section 2(b), creates a contradiction. Section 2(b) defines "Products" as including dietary supplements but also lists specific exclusions and inclusions. By narrowing the scope in Section 4(c) to only "dietary supplements", it implies that MusclePharm's right to use endorsements might be limited only to dietary supplements and not other items included in the broader definition of 'Products' in Section 2(b). This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the company's right to use endorsements suddenly becomes ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: The AS Parties shall have the right, upon at least five (5) days written notice and no more than once each Contract Year of the Term to inspect MusclePharm's books and records and all other documents and material in the possession of or under the control of MusclePharm with respect to the Licensed Products at the place or places where such records are normally retained by MusclePharm. The AS Parties shall have reasonable access thereto for such purposes and shall be permitted to be able to make copies thereof and extracts therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: The AS Parties shall have the right, upon at least five (5) days written notice and no more than once each Contract Year of the Term to inspect MusclePharm's accounting documents and all other documents and material in the possession of or under the control of MusclePharm with respect to the Licensed Products at the place or places where such records are normally retained by MusclePharm. The AS Parties shall have reasonable access thereto for such purposes and shall be permitted to be able to make copies thereof and extracts therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "books and records" to "accounting documents" in Section 11(a) creates a narrower scope of inspection. While 'books and records' typically encompasses a broad range of company documentation, 'accounting documents' specifically refers to financial records. This change could limit the AS Parties' ability to thoroughly audit MusclePharm's compliance with the agreement, as non-financial records related to Licensed Products (e.g., marketing materials, production logs) might be excluded from inspection. It creates ambiguity to what records, the AS party has access to and is entitled to see.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The AS Parties shall have the right, upon at least five (5) days written notice and no more than once each Contract Year of the Term to inspect MusclePharm's accounting documents and all other documents and material in the possession of or under the control of MusclePharm with respect to the Licensed Products at the place or places where such records are normally retained by MusclePharm. The AS Parties shall have reasonable access thereto for such purposes and shall be permitted to be able to make copies thereof and extracts therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, while narrowing the scope from 'books and records' to 'accounting documents,' does not create a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text uses the term 'accounting documents' itself. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, there isn't an in-text contradiction.
